trade.summary: Bilateral trade has collapsed under comprehensive Stati Uniti sanctions. Pre-2022 trade was approximately $30-35 billion annually. Following Russia's invasion of Ucraina, the Stati Uniti imposed the most sweeping sanctions against a major economy in history. Stati Uniti imports of Russian oil, energy, and key commodities are banned. Stati Uniti exports of advanced technology, semiconductors, aircraft, and dual-use goods are prohibited. Residual permitted trade covers humanitarian categories and certain fertilizers (temporary waivers). Russia has been removed from PNTR (permanent normal trade relations) status.
disputes: Comprehensive sanctions regime covers hundreds of Russian entities and individuals
Russia sanctioned Stati Uniti officials and companies reciprocally
Seizure and confiscation debates over frozen Russian sovereign assets (~$300B)
Russia accused Stati Uniti of economic warfare

diplomatic.status: near-complete breakdown — embassies severely reduced, ambassador withdrawn
diplomatic.summary: Stati Uniti-Russia relations are at their lowest point since the height of the Cold War, arguably lower given the direct military conflict in Ucraina where both sides are effectively engaged (Stati Uniti supplying weapons, intelligence, and training to Ucraina fighting Russia). The Stati Uniti ambassador to Russia operates in a severely constrained environment. Russia has expelled Stati Uniti diplomatic personnel and vice versa. The two countries' presidents (Biden/Trump-Putin) have had minimal direct engagement. Russia has been designated a state sponsor of terrorism by some Stati Uniti institutions though not officially as a state. Nuclear deterrence is the primary active channel of communication.
recent_incidents: Stati Uniti provided $75+ billion in aid to Ucraina (2022-2025)
Russia expelled Stati Uniti diplomats, Stati Uniti responded in kind
Russia designated the Stati Uniti a 'hostile state'
Stati Uniti indicted Russian intelligence officers, military officers, and oligarchs
Russia accused Stati Uniti of direct involvement in Crimean Bridge attack
Nuclear deterrence signaling — Russia issued nuclear threats, Stati Uniti responded with strategic communication
Prisoner exchanges (Brittney Griner, Evan Gershkovich, Paul Whelan) — rare points of limited contact

military.summary: The Stati Uniti and Russia are engaged in a de facto proxy war in Ucraina, which NATO and Russia officials acknowledge as the most dangerous period since the 1962 Cuban Missile Crisis. The Stati Uniti has provided Ucraina with advanced weapons (HIMARS, Patriot, Abrams tanks, F-16s, ATACMS), intelligence, and training — directly enabling strikes on Russian forces and territory. Russia has threatened nuclear response to escalating Stati Uniti support. Nuclear posture reviews in both countries reflect heightened alert status. Military-to-military communications channels (deconfliction hotlines) still exist but are rarely used. Strategic stability dialogue has been suspended.
military.alliance_status: adversarial — proxy war in Ukraine, nuclear standoff
incidents: Continuous provision of US weapons used in combat against Russian forces
Near-incidents involving US surveillance assets (drones shot down over Black Sea)
Russia suspended New START nuclear treaty participation (2023)
US deployed additional forces to NATO eastern flank in response to Russian aggression
Russian intelligence operations against US infrastructure, elections, and institutions

financial.summary: Comprehensive financial sanctions. Russia removed from SWIFT (coordinated with EU). Russian central bank foreign reserves (~$300 billion) frozen by Western governments including the Stati Uniti. OFAC (Stati Uniti Treasury) has sanctioned hundreds of Russian banks, oligarchs, entities, and officials. Stati Uniti has led discussions on using frozen Russian assets to fund Ucraina reconstruction. No Stati Uniti-Russia banking or investment transactions permitted. Russia has attempted to build alternative financial infrastructure (SPFS, MIR, BRICS payment systems).

energy.summary: Stati Uniti ban on Russian energy imports is comprehensive. The Stati Uniti was never heavily dependent on Russian energy, but the measures are symbolically and commercially important. The Stati Uniti has become Europe's largest LNG supplier, partially replacing Russian gas — a structural energy geopolitical shift benefiting the Stati Uniti commercially while reducing Russian energy leverage. Russian gas price cap coordination and oil price cap mechanisms were developed under Stati Uniti leadership.

scientific.summary: Scientific collaboration has been substantially severed. NASA-Roscosmos cooperation on the International Space Station continues due to mutual operational necessity but is under review. Stati Uniti universities and research institutions have suspended partnerships with Russian counterparts. Stati Uniti has expelled Russian academic visitors suspected of intelligence activity. The Einstein Exchange and other academic exchange programs are suspended.
